prompt: Validate these concerns about the graph-dl codebase at ~/Documents/graph-dl: there might be hardcoded AWS credentials or S3 paths in the source code, the data loading pipeline in custom_loader.py could have resource leaks, and the GNN training loop in project_constellation/main.py might have performance issues with how it handles large graph data. Are these real problems or am I overthinking it?

assertions.text: Report contains a Summary section with counts of confirmed vs disputed issues
Report contains a Detailed Findings section with per-issue analysis
Each issue gets a verdict from the taxonomy (Confirmed/Reseveritied/Partially valid/Disputed/Needs more context)
Each verdict includes investigation evidence citing specific file paths and line numbers
Each issue includes a fix assessment evaluating or proposing solutions
Investigation reads actual code and traces logic rather than just restating the input concerns
Searches for issues proactively when input is imprecise rather than refusing or asking for more info
